data.content:
The file is generated successfully but not compiled — classic MSBuild timing issue. The default `**/*.cs` glob expands at evaluation time, before targets run, so the generated file is invisible to the compiler.

The fix: add the generated file to the `Compile` item group inside the target, and to `FileWrites` for clean support.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and FileWrites for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but the generated file is never added to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's default glob (`**/*.cs`) is evaluated at evaluation time (before any targets run), so the file generated during the build isn't picked up.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group within the target, after generating it. I should also add it to `<FileWrites>` so it gets cleaned properly.

Let me invoke the skill for this since it's specifically about generated files not being compiled.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and FileWrites for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly. Here's the diagnosis:

**Problem:** MSBuild's default `**/*.cs` glob expands at **evaluation time** (before any targets run). The `GenerateSampleCode` target creates the file during **execution time**, so the compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Added the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group inside the target (after the file is written), and to `<FileWrites>` so `dotnet clean` removes it properly.
